person.name: Elizabeth Maud Bromwich

memoryArtifacts.headline: Eng.; Herefordshire, Bodenham; St. Michaels school

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Thomas Bromwich b. c 1373

Mother Alice Oldcastle b. c 1368

Elizabeth Bromwich was born circa 1391. She married Walter Devereux, Esq., son of Sir Walter Devereux, Sheriff of Herefordshire, Constable of Builth Castle and Agnes Crophull, circa 1410 at of Weobley & Bodenham, Herefordshire, England; They had 1 son (Sir Walter).

«b»Family«/b»
Walter Devereux, Esq. b. c 1387, d. 1420

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Sir Walter Devereux, Sheriff of Herefordshire & Gloucestershire, Constable of Wigmore, Keeper of Leominster b. c 1412, d. 22 Apr 1459

2.) Cicily Devereux b. c 1415, d. c 1434

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Maud Bromwich於1380年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里時，她的父親Thomas Bromwich40歲，母親Elynor Pryce35歲。她於1409年與Walter Devereux在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1423年在英國賀福爾波登哈姆享年43歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bromwich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from West Bromwich (Staffs) or Castle and Little Bromwich (Warwicks).

variants.name: Bromage
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Worcestershire): habitational name from West Bromwich in Staffordshire, or Castle and Little Bromwich in Warwickshire, named in Old English with brōm ‘broom’ (the shrub) + wīc ‘outlying dairy farm’. Compare Brumage .

variants.name: Brimage
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Brumage  . This name is comparatively rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Maud Bromwich was born in 1380, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Bromwich, was 40 and her mother, Elynor Pryce, was 35. She married Walter Devereux in 1409, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1423, in Bodenham, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Maud Bromwich est née en 1380, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Bromwich, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Elynor Pryce, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Walter Devereux en 1409, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1423, à Bodenham, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Maud Bromwich 1380 in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Bromwich 40 und ihre Mutter Elynor Pryce 35. Sie hat 1409 in Herefordshire, England Walter Devereux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1423 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Bodenham, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Maud Bromwich è nata il nel 1380 nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Bromwich, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Elynor Pryce, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Walter Devereux nel 1409, nel Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1423, nel Bodenham, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Maud Bromwichが1380年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Bromwichは40歳，母Elynor Pryceは35歳であった。1409年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでWalter Devereuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1423年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアボデンハムで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Maud Bromwich(이)가 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Bromwich(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elynor Pryce(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1409년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Walter Devereux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1423년 영국 허퍼드실르 보데함에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Maud Bromwich nasceu em 1380, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Bromwich, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Elynor Pryce, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter Devereux em 1409, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1423, em Bodenham, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Maud Bromwich родилась в 1380 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Bromwich было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elynor Pryce, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Devereux в 1409 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1423 г. в местности боденам, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Maud Bromwich nació en 1380, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Bromwich, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Elynor Pryce, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Walter Devereux en 1409, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1423, en Bodenham, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años.
